title: caloncoba subtomentosa
intro: Caloncoba subtomentosa, also known as the 'Black Cinnamon', is a tropical tree native to the tropical regions of West and Central Africa. Its bark and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Caloncoba subtomentosa include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as having antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Caloncoba subtomentosa include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and other medicinal products.
